name: Côn Đảo National Park Tourist Office

reviews.author: Tony
reviews.text: A place to visit. Buy tickets 50.000 each from the office. Recommend walking up to the entrance, about 300m (like the staff recommends aswell). There are aggressive monkeys where the trail begin and you gove your tickets. I would not want monkey shit on my mopeds seat as we saw.. Lots of fauna, saw bats, monkeys, countless lizards, couple of Shakespeare, squirrels etc. Keep your eyes off the road as well to spot them. The first t section comes to the cave/altar. The second place where you can turn is ancient tree, take a look it is really close. And a Y section to waterfall and beach and right to a beach, there is also a restaurant. The waterfall is not that remarkable during dry season (omg who would have guessed.....) but on rainy season i bet it is beautiful. Overall a nice easy hike with alot to see.

reviews.author: Tia Rlin
reviews.text: not worth it to be honest. we expected something more than just paved roads that lead to rocky and dirty beaches. the hike itself was okay, but there was not really anything beautiful to see on the way, except some monkeys and lizards. You can't swim cause the first beach is full of rocks and shallow water and the second beach is the same, but even worth. Also, a lot of trash gets washed up the shore. For a national park with entrance fee that should be better maintained. the walk up to the cave is not worth it, it's just a tiny cave with an altar and Jesus. the waterfall stream was non existent, but maybe it's better in wet season. I wouldn't go again. no nice views, no nice beaches, and just lots of sweat.

reviews.author: Tony Day
reviews.text: There is an official entrance (ticket booth with big National Park signage) that is not on the map about 250 North on the same road as the visor centre. You can buy tickets there at the booth and walk up to 3km out to a cave, beach and waterfall and marked stone paths. The cave is a small religious monument. There are 2 beaches and we only went to one (Bang Beach) which had nice looking water but was stoney with no real shade. The waterfall is weather dependent of course, it was completely dry when we went. It's very hot in the forest but we saw lots of wildlife: at least two giant black squirrels (look for them sleeping on horizontal branches), many other black squirrels (but not sure if these were the same, they had smaller tails and were full black), a monitor lizard (about 75cm in length), a troop of monkeys, and many others smaller lizards and birds (and possibly a smaller primate / mammal. We also unexpectedly encountered wild boar which scared the life of us. We heard them and then saw them scatter. At least 10 individuals with several youngsters. The group of monkeys that we encountered at the entrance became aggressive so best not to linger or access your backpacks around them. You can ride a scooter easily to the entrance either by going all the way around the island, or by cutting through from the town past the national park visit centre. The walk can be challenging in the heat and humidity. We were sweating buckets by the end of it. The descent to the beach and subsequent climb back up was hard work.

reviews.author: Justin Lui
reviews.text: I spent only a few hours and did not explore outside of ONE trail but it is a very solid hike. If I had more time to explore, I would have. It seems very promising — similar to a less dense Hong Kong or Taiwan with less robust mountains

reviews.author: Gilbert Perron
reviews.text: Slippery trail. Limited views. Nothing spectacular. It cost 50k pax.

reviews.author: Glady Rose G
reviews.text: A great place to hike! The path going to your desired destination is clean and easy to pass through. I suggest going in groups because there are not a lot of people that trek this place. Make sure that you bring enough water, phone battery, and mobile data. There are are 3 place you can hike. Please refer to the brochure at the ticket office.

reviews.author: Erika Andrea Carranza Rojas
reviews.text: Around 6,5 km walking where you can visit some beautiful beaches that aren't so swimmable because of the rocks, but if you enjoy hiking and nature it's a great plan. We didn't have the chance to see any monkeys or birds as we expected but anyways it was great, 4/5 because in some areas of the beach and on the way there was some trash, and I guess maybe some signs or recommendation for people going would be good to remind them that they can't treat beautiful places as trash bags...

reviews.author: aj
reviews.text: Côn Đảo National Park, nestled in the captivating archipelago of Côn Đảo in Vietnam, is a nature lover's paradise. Spanning lush tropical forests, pristine beaches, and rugged mountains, the park offers a mesmerizing blend of biodiversity and scenic beauty. Visitors are treated to a unique experience, encountering diverse wildlife, including rare species like the endemic Côn Đảo black squirrel. The well-maintained trails provide opportunities for immersive hikes, revealing breathtaking panoramas. Preservation efforts have successfully conserved the park's ecological balance. With its serene ambiance and rich ecological tapestry, Côn Đảo National Park stands as a testament to Vietnam's commitment to environmental conservation and offers a memorable escape into the wonders of untouched nature.

reviews.author: Amy Passafiume
reviews.text: Absolutely stunning park full of beautiful wildlife. We visited with tour guides who had a lot of information and knew the path/trails very well. We hiked 6km in flip flops (because that’s all we brought on vacation) - so it’s doable but proper shoes would be ideal! Make sure to bring lots of water but the hike is well worth your time and energy to experience the relatively untouched beauty of Côn Đảo!

reviews.author: Matthias Kreier
reviews.text: I took the 6.5 kilometer walk through the park from southwest entrance near the ticket office to the other end close to the airport. The trail leads to the twin peaks of the highest mountain on Con Dao at 502m. That's why it took me 3 hours. There is nothing to see, you are surrounded by rain forrest. They still build the road, it is made suitabe for motorbikes to 220m height. The bridge at 280m height is under construction in December 2020. Several trees are labeled on the path, its quite and you are protected from the sun. Even on 500m height you can spot crabs!

reviews.author: Han Nguyen
reviews.text: a good place for hiking in the forest, beautiful waterfall, beautiful beaches, beautiful ocean!

reviews.author: Solari Udine
reviews.text: If you like to enter any place in Con Dao national park, you should buy a 60k ticket which will be available 12 hours per a day. Some places you may like will be Đầm Tre lagoon(bay), Ông Đụng beach, Hòn Bảy Cạnh, Bàng beach, Đất Thắm beach, Hoài Dương water fall etc. Because Đầm Tre bay is a long way and need some time to get there(you may need at least 4-5 hours for the trekking and snorkeling over there) so you should select your moving lines reasonably. Hòn Bảy Cạnh islet tour will also need some time. I recommend at least you will need 2 days to go to the national park places

reviews.author: Belinda Smith
reviews.text: Amazing experience in the National Park. Buy your ticket and fill in the COVID declaration at the ticket office in the park. Then follow the clearly signposted paths to explore the rainforest. We saw monkeys and the endemic to Con Dao black squirrels plus plenty of insect life. Some steep areas but not too challenging.

reviews.author: Renate Heitzer
reviews.text: hidden treasures, small "river" coming down from the national park... (beach next to the luxury resorts) still some lessons to be learned (but who are we Europeans to judge)

reviews.author: Fred
reviews.text: I am surprised not a lot of people mention how slippery the path is. It's a proper stone path, which theoretically should be good. But these paths were covered by moss which made it a slip hazard. Wearing hiking boots with proper grip doesn't help either. Walking on the dirt, even wet and muddy dirt, has better grip than walking on the stone path. People need to step carefully. The hike itself is not as rewarding. It leads to a cave which is okay at best. A tree, which is "not bad" but not "great". The best part of the hike is the beaches. The beach is nice and beautiful. But there are also other beaches around the island that are as beautiful. I like the beach here because there's almost no people around and you basically have the beach to yourself. Sometimes there's only one or two people with you. The waterfall stream is underwhelming. The "waterfall" is pretty high but it's coming from a small stream. If you have extra time to explore, do it. But if not, this one can be skipped.

reviews.author: KHO MONICA
reviews.text: It's a big area to hike and explore, need good stamina climbing to Holy Cave, Bang Waterfall, and Dat Tham Beach.

reviews.author: Monika Vu Thi
reviews.text: The hike was great, it’s in the jungle so luckily there’s wind sometime. Don’t forget to check if there’s a enough water by the time you’ll arrive at the beach because from the noon, there’s a lot less water than in the morning so its better to hike from the morning or after rain so at least you can see the waterfalls. We arrived at the beach around 12:00PM and the water already moved so far we couldn’t get far enough to swim. You can bring your own bottle of water but I recommend you to keep the bottle and throw it in the bins outside the jungle.

reviews.author: Megan Leung
reviews.text: [Review for foreigners who are looking to sign up for a turtle tour] I wasn't able to use my non-Vietnamese credit card for ordering online, so instead I went into the office to book releasing the baby sea turtles. The tour starts at 7 AM, and we were supported by Andy. He is a fantastic guide, and with his English fluency, I got to learn about the turtles, conservation efforts, and he was able to answer my specific questions about species and lifespans. The tour includes a stop at Coconut Island, followed by a snorkel in gorgeous coral. (Hot tip: wear a long sleeve rash guard and full leg cover to protect yourself from the jellyfish!) On the boat ride home, we got to enjoy some rambutan. My only regret is not booking the overnight stay to watch the sea turtles lay eggs! (The night before they got 10!) Thank you Andy for a lovely morning! I hope tourism like this can help educate folks on what we can do to help protect these beautiful creatures.

reviews.author: Witek Bielak
reviews.text: Good hike but limited views as the forest\jungle is super dense

reviews.author: Carlos
reviews.text: Nice natural place, come here very early so you can go to the beaches on the other side. It’s kind of a long walk.

reviews.author: Sally Webb
reviews.text: Fabulous, easy (6km return) paved hike - we went with 3 generations and yes the steps down to the beach were steep but very doable and no the beach wasn’t as dirty as other reviews say. (The rubbish is what gets washed up at high tide but it was more or less all in one place by the bottom of the steps). We did the ‘side hikes’ to the cave and the Heritage Tree (but not the waterfall as it’s not rainy season.) Do pay your 50k entrance fee - why wouldn’t you?! Good toilets in Visitor Centre where you buy the tickets then walk/scooter to the hike entrance UP the road.

reviews.author: manual
reviews.text: It's really nice to walk through the jungle! But what we really don't understand is the way nature is treated, even in this "protected" zone. Ways are under construction (2019), which is OK, but cement bags (plastic!) lay around everywhere instead of being gathered and disposed. Motorbikes drive on the ways to get the cement from a to b. No chance to see animals or birds with that noise. Unfortunately a disappointing trip. Please Guys, keep your plastic! Don't litter it everywhere on the beautiful island, especially not in a national park! Besides the wrong acting and littering tourists (we saw so many) the "official" workers should also be trained in that!

reviews.author: Hans Peter
reviews.text: The track through the jungle is nice, but the beach is not worth the way. If you wanna go, do it for the walk itself or the waterfall. But don’t hope for a nice beach :)

reviews.author: Dg Khoa (BigCatDgk)
reviews.text: Wonderful and good service, but i don’t see the parking lot for motorbike.

reviews.author: The MY World
reviews.text: It's national park. You can visit several beach and forest.

reviews.author: erica bertugli
reviews.text: They can provide a few information about the hikes, but they don't speak much English, so the conversation goes with Google translate. For most of the hikes you need to pay about 50k per person per day. Inside the park there is lot of plastic and garbage, we did the hike to the lagoon and both the beach at the beginning of the trail and the lagoon itself were pretty dirty.

reviews.author: Chris Wallace
reviews.text: This is the place to buy your tickets if you're planning to hike in the National Park. Tickets are 50,000VND per person. We dealt with a lovely lady called Huỳnh Anh who was really helpful and recommended the best routes to go on. The National Park is beautiful and well worth a visit.

reviews.author: Manuel Diekmann
reviews.text: It’s a beautiful park! You can enjoy the nature with lots of different animals. Unfortunately, garbage was laying around in some corners. It took us just 20 min to fill two huge bags full of garbage. Also wondering if they are just burning the trash. In summary, with better garbage management this is a must see for everyone.

reviews.author: Stuart Jenkinson
reviews.text: As said before, be very careful when climbing the trail. The moss can be slippery. Someone had also discarded some half eaten fruit on a very steep section of the trail for some reason, (it was also green, so blended in) that I slipped on on my way back. If I had fallen, it would have been quite nasty.
